# SmallC-Interpreter

> A Small-C interactive interpreter for system software education.

SmallC-Interpreter is a fully-featured interactive interpreter for the Small-C programming language, a strict subset of ISO C. It provides a REPL environment where users can enter Small-C code for immediate execution or load complete source files for batch execution.

## Key Features

- Interactive REPL with `sc>` prompt and multi-line input detection
- Recursive-descent parser with 13-level operator precedence
- Tree-walking interpreter with simulated byte-addressable memory
- 23 built-in functions (I/O, string, math, utility)
- Program buffer management (LOAD, SAVE, LIST, EDIT, DELETE, INSERT, APPEND)
- Execution tracing (TRACE ON/OFF) and state inspection (VARS, FUNCS)
- Comprehensive error handling with syntax and runtime error messages

## Supported Small-C Features

- Data types: int (32-bit), char (8-bit), pointers (int*, char*)
- Control flow: if/else, while, for, do/while, break, continue, return, switch/case
- Functions with recursion, one-dimensional arrays, pointer arithmetic
- #define preprocessor, C-style comments

## Technical Details

- Language: Python 3.10+
- No third-party dependencies
- Architecture: Source → Preprocessor → Lexer → Parser → AST → Interpreter
- Entry point: python3 main.py

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/canslab1/SmallC-Interpreter
- CANS Lab: https://canslab1.github.io/
